2026-04-17T07:03:31.4023264Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4023801Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4024239Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4024604Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4024917Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4025156Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4025325Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4025514Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4025689Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4025924Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-17T07:03:31.4026413Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-17T07:03:31.4026671Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

Use the endCursor from the previous page's PageInfo for GraphQL APIs.","type":"string"},"direction":{"description":"Order direction. If provided, the 'orderBy' also needs to be provided.","enum":["ASC","DESC"],"type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Filter by labels","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"orderBy":{"description":"Order issues by field. If provided, the 'direction' also needs to be provided.","enum":["CREATED_AT","UPDATED_AT","COMMENTS"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Repository owner","type":"string"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"repo":{"description":"Repository name","type":"string"},"since":{"description":"Filter by date (ISO 8601 timestamp)","type":"string"},"state":{"description":"Filter by state, by default both open and closed issues are returned when not provided","enum":["OPEN","CLOSED"],"type":"string"}},"required":["owner","repo"],"type":"object"},"name":"list_issues"},{"annotations":{"readOnlyHint":true,"title":"Search issues"},"description":"Search for issues in GitHub repositories using issues search syntax already scoped to is:issue","icons":[{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"light"},{"mimeType":"image/png","src":"data:image/png;base64,","theme":"dark"}],"inputSchema":{"properties":{"order":{"description":"Sort order","enum":["asc","desc"],"type":"string"},"owner":{"description":"Optional repository owner. If provided with repo, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"page":{"description":"Page number for pagination (min 1)","minimum":1,"type":"number"},"perPage":{"description":"Results per page for pagination (min 1, max 100)","maximum":100,"minimum":1,"type":"number"},"query":{"description":"Search query using GitHub issues search syntax","type":"string"},"repo":{"description":"Optional repository name. If provided with owner, only issues for this repository are listed.","type":"string"},"sort":{"description":"Sort field by number of matches of categories, defaults to best match","enum":["comments","reactions","reactions-+1","reactions--1","reactions-smile","reactions-thinking_face","reactions-heart","reactions-tada","interactions","created","updated"],"type":"string"}},"required":["query"],"type":"object"},"name":"search_issues"}]}}}
2026-04-17T07:07:25.5384606Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:04:37.601999027Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"perPage":10,"query":"repo:Azure/azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"search_repositories"}}}

2026-04-17T07:07:25.5390121Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:04:37.614846706Z","direction":"OUT","type":"REQUEST","server_id":"github","method":"tools/call","payload":{"jsonrpc":"2.0","method":"tools/call","params":{"arguments":{"issue_number":"58266","method":"get","owner":"Azure","repo":"azure-sdk-for-net"},"name":"issue_read"}}}
2026-04-17T07:07:25.5426077Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:04:37.824657261Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58266,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document chat isolation, metadata constraints, ID validation, and observability features added in beta.2\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added several significant features and breaking changes to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header): all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for a response must include the same key used at creation; mismatch returns 404\\n- **Metadata constraint validation**: maps limited to 16 keys, key length ≤ 64, value length ≤ 512\\n- **Malformed response ID validation** for both the `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs outbound Foundry storage API calls with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers\\n- **Breaking changes to error shapes**: POST-delete operations now return 404 (was 400), `invalid_request_error` type for 400 errors, cancel-after-delete returns 404\\n- **HttpClient instrumentation** added to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` for outbound trace correlation\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key** (`x-agent-chat-isolation-key`) is not documented anywhere in the README. The `ResponseContext.Isolation` property is mentioned once without explanation. Users need to understand when and how to use this header for multi-tenant scenarios\\n- **Metadata constraints** (16 keys max, 64-char keys, 512-char values) are not mentioned in the README. Users will get 400 errors without any hint of what limits apply\\n- **Response ID format requirements** are not documented. Users creating custom integrations need to know that IDs must match a specific format (`caresp_` prefix with exact length) or requests will be rejected with 400\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`** is not documented in the README Logging section. Users building production deployments need to know this policy exists and what it logs\\n- **Breaking changes in error shapes** are not reflected in the README Troubleshooting section. The 400 → 404 change for post-delete scenarios and the updated error type names will surprise users upgrading from beta.1\\n- **OpenTelemetry / HttpClient instrumentation** added to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` is not reflected in the Responses README observability guidance\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` (edit)\\n\\nAdd a new **Chat Isolation** subsection under **Key concepts**, immediately after the **ResponseContext** subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat Isolation\\n\\nWhen a response is created with the `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header, all subsequent GET,\\nCancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls for that response must include the same key. A mismatched or\\nmissing key returns **404 Not Found**, indistinguishable from a response that does not exist. This\\nprevents cross-chat information leakage in multi-tenant deployments.\\n\\n```\\n// Creating with a chat isolation key (set the header on the client request)\\n// x-agent-chat-isolation-key=[REDACTED] ResponseContext.Isolation contains the key extracted from the inbound request\\nvar isolationKey = context.Isolation?.IsolationKey;\\n```\\n\\nNo enforcement applies to responses created without this header.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\nAdd a **Metadata constraints** note to the **Key concepts → ResponseContext** section (or as its own subsection):\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Metadata constraints\\n\\nMetadata maps attached to a response must satisfy:\\n- At most **16 key-value pairs**\\n- Key length ≤ **64 characters**\\n- Value length ≤ **512 characters**\\n\\nRequests that exceed these limits are rejected with **400 Bad Request**.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\nAdd an **Input validation** subsection under **Key concepts**:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Input validation\\n\\nResponse IDs (both the `response_id` path parameter and the `previous_response_id` body field)\\nmust conform to the expected format (`caresp_` prefix, fixed total length). Malformed IDs are\\nrejected with **400 Bad Request** and a descriptive error message.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\nUpdate the **Logging** subsection under **Troubleshooting** to mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`:\\n\\nReplace the existing logging paragraph with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\nEnable logging in your ASP.NET Core application to diagnose issues.\\n\\nOutbound calls to the Foundry storage layer are logged by `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`, a\\nbuilt-in Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy. Each attempt is logged with HTTP method, URI,\\nstatus code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`,\\n`x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`). Successful calls are logged at `Information` level; HTTP\\nerrors at `Warning`; exceptions at `Error`. No credentials or PII are included.\\n\\nThe `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` package also registers `AddHttpClientInstrumentation()` for\\nOpenTelemetry, exporting outbound HTTP client spans so distributed traces include the full call\\nchain through the Foundry storage layer.\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\nUpdate the **Troubleshooting → Common errors** section to reflect the beta.2 breaking changes:\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet points with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request** (creation/update): The request body failed validation. Possible causes:\\n  - `input` items are malformed or `model` is invalid\\n  - `previous_response_id` does not match the expected ID format\\n  - Metadata map exceeds 16 keys, or a key/value exceeds the length limits (64 / 512 characters)\\n  - Response ID in path parameter is malformed\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired, or was deleted. This status is\\n  also returned when a chat isolation key is present and mismatched (intentionally indistinguishable from not-found).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state (`completed`,\\n  `failed`, or `cancelled`).\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or GET after delete): The response was deleted, created with\\n  `store=false`, or a non-background response is still in-flight.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new code snippets are needed for the changes described above; they are prose descriptions of behavior and constraints. The existing snippet infrastructure does not need updating.\\n\\nIf a sample demonstrating chat isolation is desired in the future, it should be placed in\\n`sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/tests/Samples/Sample_ChatIsolation.cs` with a\\n`#region Snippet:Responses_ReadMe_ChatIsolation` / `#endregion` block. The corresponding README\\nplaceholder would be:\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README already contains all required sections in correct order:\\n1. ✅ Getting started (Install, Prerequisites, Configure the server)\\n2. ✅ Key concepts — needs the three new subsections (Chat Isolation, Metadata constraints, Input validation)\\n3. ✅ Examples\\n4. ⚠️ Troubleshooting — Logging and Common errors subsections need updates\\n5. ✅ Next steps\\n6. ✅ Contributing\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README is unchanged (no new snippet placeholders added in this PR)\\n4. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n\\nNo public API surface was changed by this documentation-only update, so `Export-API.ps1` is not needed.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-17T07:07:25.5589546Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-17T07:05:00.97502489Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"total_count\":909,\"incomplete_results\":false,\"items\":[{\"id\":4090517017,\"number\":57183,\"state\":\"closed\",\"state_reason\":\"completed\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[BUG] Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent NullReferenceException after successful streamed response finalization\",\"body\":\"## Bug description\\nWhen using `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` through Microsoft Agent Framework streaming APIs, a chat run can complete successfully, return the full assistant answer, and then crash during stream finalization/disposal with a `System.NullReferenceException`.\\n\\nThe exception originates inside the Azure SDK telemetry/finalization path:\\n`Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(...)`\\n\\n## Package and environment\\n- Package: `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent`\\n- Package version: `1.0.0-preview.260311.1`\\n- App shape: .NET 10 console app using Microsoft Agent Framework with Azure Foundry persistent agents\\n- OS: Windows\\n- Auth=[REDACTED] Streaming path: `AIAgent.RunStreamingAsync(...)`\\n\\n## Expected behavior\\nAfter the streamed answer completes, enumeration/disposal should finish cleanly.\\n\\n## Actual behavior\\nThe full assistant answer is returned to the caller, but the process crashes afterward during stream cleanup/final telemetry processing.\\n\\n## Reproduction notes\\n1. Create a persistent Foundry agent and connect with `PersistentAgentsClient` / `ChatClientAgent`.\\n2. Wrap the agent with Agent Framework OpenTelemetry instrumentation.\\n3. Call `RunStreamingAsync(...)` and enumerate the response stream to completion.\\n4. Observe that, after the final assistant output is emitted, the SDK throws `NullReferenceException` during finalization.\\n\\n## Minimal observed output\\n```text\\nassistant(stream)\\u003e \\u003cfull answer omitted\\u003e\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[update] role=assistant, messageId=..., author=..., responseId=...\\n[content:UsageContent] Microsoft.Extensions.AI.UsageContent\\nUnhandled exception. System.NullReferenceException: Object reference not set to an instance of an object.\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.JsonModelWriteCore(Utf8JsonWriter writer, ModelReaderWriterOptions options)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.System.ClientModel.Primitives.IJsonModel\\u003cAzure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall\\u003e.Write(...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.ModelSerializationExtensions.WriteObjectValue[T](...)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.ToRequestContent()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.GetToolCallAttributes(RunStepToolCall toolCall)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.ProcessToolCalls(RunStepToolCallDetails toolCallDetails)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.RecordRunStepEventAttributes(RunStep runStep, Boolean stream)\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.End()\\n   at Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent.Telemetry.OpenTelemetryScope.Dispose()\\n```\\n\\n## Suspected root cause\\nThe generated serializer for `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` appears to iterate `OpenAPI` without a null check.\\n\\nRelevant file: `sdk/ai/Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent/src/Generated/RunStepOpenAPIToolCall.Serialization.cs`\\n\\n## Impact\\n- The user-visible answer is already complete, but the client process still terminates.\\n- This makes streamed chat runs unreliable in production.\\n- The issue appears tied to SDK finalization/telemetry, not application rendering.\\n\\n## Workaround currently used\\nApplication-side workaround: catch this very specific `NullReferenceException` after the stream completes and preserve the completed answer.\\n\\n## Request\\nPlease confirm whether this is a known bug in `Azure.AI.Agents.Persistent` preview and whether a null-safe fix should be added around `RunStepOpenAPIToolCall` serialization / telemetry attribute generation.\",\"author_association\":\"MEMBER\",\"user\":{\"login\":\"brandonh-msft\",\"id\":20270743,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjIwMjcwNzQz\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/20270743?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/brandonh-msft\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/brandonh-msft/subscriptions\"},\"labels\":[{\"id\":1069434707,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/repos/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/labels/Service%20Attention\",\"name\":\"Service
